status.integration_note: This isolated art package does not modify Core asset records, content hashes, renderer behavior, collision, attack origins, telegraphs, route gates, or normal-route authority.

selection_rationale: Core requires six normal enemies at M03. Existing candidate art covers Drowned Pilgrim, Bell Reed, and Chain Sentry; this pack covers the remaining Mire Leech, Bell Acolyte, and Choir Skull records so the complete six-enemy roster can enter native readability review.
design_authority: Gravebound_Production_GDD_v1_Canonical.md:ENC-001, ENC-002, ART-001, ART-002, ART-004, ART-005, ART-020, ART-030

input_images.role: existing project palette, pixel-cluster, framing, and scale reference only; not an edit target

generation.prompt: Create one exact three-column sprite atlas containing, from left to right, Mire Leech, Bell Acolyte, and Choir Skull. Each cell contains exactly one isolated normal-enemy idle seed. Match the reference's polished 2D dark-fantasy pixel art, shape-first silhouettes, restrained detail, top-down three-quarter framing, hard-edged pixel clusters, muted wet-stone/tarnished-brass/ash/bone/moss palette, identical apparent scale, generous padding, and bottom-center alignment. Mire Leech is a low nonhumanoid charcoal segmented swamp rusher with a bone-toothed lamprey mouth. Bell Acolyte is a slim upright ranged parish acolyte with a bone mask, ash-gray vestments, low handbell, and hooked censer staff, clearly distinct from Drowned Pilgrim. Choir Skull is an oversized floating skull in a sparse black iron choir halo with two brass chimes, no body, flame, or smoke. Use a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background. No shadows, scenery, attack poses, projectiles, telegraphs, loot beams, exits, safe-zone rings, labels, dividers, text, UI, borders, logos, watermarks, or subject overlap.

postprocess.normalization: Split the source into equal thirds, trim each nonempty alpha bound with 12 source-pixel padding, nearest-neighbor fit into a 44-pixel content box, horizontally center, and bottom-align on a transparent 48x48 canvas.
postprocess.collision_authority: None. Authoritative collision, hurtbox, attack, telegraph, locomotion, and state-machine data remain in Core content and simulation records.

validation.automated: All three runtime candidates are RGBA 48x48, have transparent corners, nonempty alpha coverage, and bottom-center anchors. Runtime alpha bounds remain distinct and fully inside the canvas.
validation.visual: Inspected at 48px, nearest-neighbor 96px, and grayscale. The low horizontal leech, tall narrow acolyte, and circular floating skull remain distinguishable without color. No projectile, telegraph, loot, exit, safe-zone, or scenery cue is baked into the sprites.
validation.remaining_gate: Before registry or content-hash changes, review the candidates in an optimized native Core room against the actual player sprite, wet-stone background, enemy telegraphs, hostile projectiles, and 1280x720/1920x1080 standard and reduced-effects captures. Confirm scale, anchor, grayscale hierarchy, collision implication, attack-origin implication, and Bell Acolyte separation from Drowned Pilgrim.

license_provenance: Project-generated original material using OpenAI built-in image generation. Existing project imagery was used only as an in-project visual style reference. Commercial-rights and registry review remain required before shipping.
